news.title: Elon Musk misled Twitter investors while trying to get out of acquisition, jury says
news.summary: At the time, Musk had tweeted that Twitter had too many bots, which is why he was trying to renege on his commitment to buy the company.

news.title: Microsoft rolls back some of its Copilot AI bloat on Windows
news.summary: The company is reducing Copilot entry points on Windows, starting with Photos, Widgets, Notepad, and other apps.

news.title: Amid legal turmoil, Kalshi is temporarily banned in Nevada
news.summary: It's the latest escalation in a regulatory battle over the future of prediction markets.

news.title: Pinterest CEO calls on governments to ban social media for users under 16
news.summary: Pinterest’s chief compares social media to tobacco and alcohol, saying children need similar protections.

news.title: Jury finds Elon Musk’s ‘stupid tweets’ caused Twitter investors’ losses
news.summary: A California jury determined that Elon Musk misled Twitter investors before making a $44 billion deal to buy the company in 2022, reports CNBC. news.title: An automated moderation error left Tumblr users panicked
news.summary: Tumblr users were left scrambling on Wednesday after dozens of accounts were banned in the same afternoon by an automated system. Numerous users contacted The Verge about the incident, claiming that the wave of bans disproportionately seemed to impact accounts run by users who identify as trans wome

news.title: A new Nintendo Switch 2 could be the poster child for replaceable batteries
news.summary: Nintendo plans to release a Switch 2 revision in the European Union that will let users replace their own batteries, Nikkei reports. The current version of the Switch 2 has a glued-in battery. But Nintendo apparently plans to make the change in order to comply with EU rules going into effect in Febr

news.title: Future Sony PlayStation games will use AI to imagine new frames

news.title: Windows 11 is finally getting a movable taskbar
news.summary: It's taken almost five years, but Microsoft is finally bringing back the ability to move the Windows taskbar to the top or sides of your screen. news.title: Jury finds Musk owes damages to Twitter investors for his tweets
news.summary: The verdict, while not a complete loss, could still cost him billions.

news.title: You're likely already infected with a brain-eating virus you've never heard of
news.summary: Fatal brain infection was thought to be from profound immune suppression. Not anymore.

news.title: Once again, ULA can't deliver when the US military needs a satellite in orbit
news.summary: ULA's Vulcan launch vehicle is grounded after a solid rocket booster anomaly last month.

news.title: Microsoft keeps insisting that it's deeply committed to the quality of Windows 11
news.summary: "Reducing unnecessary Copilot entry points" is one of Microsoft's action items.

news.title: Writer denies it, but publisher pulls horror novel after multiple allegations of AI use
news.summary: One of the first controversies of its kind.

news.title: Anthropic Denies It Could Sabotage AI Tools During War
news.summary: The Department of Defense alleges the AI developer could manipulate models in the middle of war. Company executives argue that’s impossible.

news.title: There Aren’t a Lot of Reasons to Get Excited About a New Amazon Smartphone
news.summary: The company is reportedly building a new AI-powered mobile device. If Amazon follows through on the plan, experts warn it would be next to impossible to break into a crowded market.

news.title: ‘A Rigged and Dangerous Product’: The Wildest Week for Prediction Markets Yet
news.summary: As the prediction market boom continues, backlash is growing, too, with Arizona filing criminal charges against Kalshi and public outcry after Polymarket traders threatened a journalist.

news.title: A Top Democrat Is Urging Colleagues to Support Trump’s Spy Machine
news.summary: Congressman Jim Himes claims a sweeping surveillance authority should stay intact because he hasn't seen abuses by Kash Patel's FBI, according to internal messaging obtained by WIRED.

news.title: Gamers Hate Nvidia's DLSS 5. Developers Aren’t Crazy About It, Either
news.summary: Nvidia’s new AI upscaling gaming technology struck gamers as uncanny and off-putting. Developers don't seem to like it, either, but it could be “the default” in a few years.
